6	import scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer
7	import chisel3._
8	import chisel3.experimental._
9	import chisel3.experimental.hierarchy.core.{Clone, Definition, Hierarchy, ImportDefinitionAnnotation, Instance}
10	import chisel3.properties.Class
11	import chisel3.internal.binding._
12	import chisel3.internal.firrtl.ir._
13	import chisel3.internal.firrtl.Converter
14	import chisel3.internal.naming._
15	import _root_.firrtl.annotations.{Annotation, ComponentName, IsMember, ModuleName, Named, ReferenceTarget}
16	import _root_.firrtl.annotations.AnnotationUtils.validComponentName
17	import _root_.firrtl.{annoSeqToSeq, AnnotationSeq}
18	import _root_.firrtl.renamemap.MutableRenameMap
19	import _root_.firrtl.{ir => fir}
20	import chisel3.experimental.dataview.{reify, reifyIdentityView, reifySingleTarget}
21	import chisel3.internal.Builder.Prefix
22	import logger.{LazyLogging, LoggerOption}
23	
24	import scala.collection.mutable
25	import scala.annotation.{nowarn, tailrec}
26	import java.io.File
27	import scala.util.control.NonFatal
28	import chisel3.ChiselException
29	import logger.LoggerOptions
30	
31	private[chisel3] class Namespace(keywords: Set[String], separator: Char = '_') {
32	  // This HashMap is compressed, not every name in the namespace is present here.
33	  // If the same name is requested multiple times, it only takes 1 entry in the HashMap and the
34	  // value is incremented for each time the name is requested.
35	  // Names can be requested that collide with compressed sets of names, thus the algorithm for
36	  // checking if a name is present in the Namespace is more complex than just checking the HashMap,
37	  // see getIndex below.
38	  private val names = collection.mutable.HashMap[String, Long]()
39	  for (keyword <- keywords)
40	    names(keyword) = 1
41	
42	  @tailrec
43	  private def rename(n: String, index: Long): String = {
44	    val tryName = s"${n}${separator}${index}"
45	    if (names.contains(tryName)) {
46	      rename(n, index + 1)
47	    } else {
48	      names(n) = index + 1
49	      tryName
50	    }
51	  }
52	
53	  /** Checks if `n` ends in `_\d+` and returns the substring before `_` if so, null otherwise */
54	  // TODO can and should this be folded in to sanitize? Same iteration as the forall?
55	  private def prefix(n: String): Int = {
56	    // This is micro-optimized because it runs on every single name
57	    var i = n.size - 1
58	    while (i > 0 && n(i).isDigit) {
59	      i -= 1
60	    }
61	    // Will get i == 0 for all digits or _\d+ with empty prefix, those have no prefix so returning 0 is correct
62	    if (i >= (n.size - 1)) 0 // no digits
63	    else if (n(i) != separator) 0 // no _
64	    else i
65	  }
66	
67	  // Gets the current index for this name, None means it is not contained in the Namespace
68	  private def getIndex(elem: String): Option[Long] =
69	    names.get(elem).orElse {
70	      // This exact name isn't contained, but if we end in _<idx>, we need to check our prefix
71	      val maybePrefix = prefix(elem)
72	      if (maybePrefix == 0) None
73	      else {
74	        // If we get a prefix collision and our index is taken, we start disambiguating with _<idx>_1
75	        names
76	          .get(elem.take(maybePrefix))
77	          .filter { prefixIdx =>
78	            val ourIdx = elem.drop(maybePrefix + 1).toInt
79	            // The namespace starts disambiguating at _1 so _0 is a false collision case
80	            ourIdx != 0 && prefixIdx > ourIdx
81	          }
82	          .map(_ => 1)
83	      }
84	    }
85	
86	  def contains(elem: String): Boolean = getIndex(elem).isDefined
87	
88	  // leadingDigitOk is for use in fields of Records
89	  def name(elem: String, leadingDigitOk: Boolean = false): String = {
90	    val sanitized = sanitize(elem, leadingDigitOk)
91	    getIndex(sanitized) match {
92	      case Some(idx) => rename(sanitized, idx)
93	      case None =>
94	        names(sanitized) = 1
95	        sanitized
96	    }
97	  }
98	}
99	
100	private[chisel3] object Namespace {
101	
102	  /** Constructs an empty Namespace */
103	  def empty(separator: Char): Namespace = new Namespace(Set.empty[String], separator)
104	  def empty:                  Namespace = new Namespace(Set.empty[String])
105	}
106	
107	private[chisel3] class IdGen {
108	  private var counter = -1L
109	  def next: Long = {
110	    counter += 1
111	    counter
112	  }
113	  def value: Long = counter
114	}
115	
116	private[chisel3] trait HasId extends chisel3.InstanceId {
117	  // using nullable var for better memory usage
118	  private var _parentVar:       BaseModule = Builder.currentModule.getOrElse(null)
119	  private[chisel3] def _parent: Option[BaseModule] = Option(_parentVar)
120	  private[chisel3] def _parent_=(target: Option[BaseModule]): Unit = {
121	    _parentVar = target.getOrElse(null)
122	  }
123	
124	  private[chisel3] val _id: Long = Builder.idGen.next
125	
126	  // TODO: remove this, but its removal seems to cause a nasty Scala compiler crash.
127	  override def hashCode:          Int = super.hashCode()
128	  override def equals(that: Any): Boolean = super.equals(that)
129	
130	  // Did the user suggest a name? This overrides names suggested by the compiler plugin.
131	  private var _nameIsSuggested: Boolean = false
132	  // Contains name seed (either user suggested or generated by the plugin).
133	  private var suggested_seedVar: String = null // using nullable var for better memory usage
134	  private def suggested_seed:    Option[String] = Option(suggested_seedVar)
135	
136	  // Prefix for use in naming
137	  // - Defaults to prefix at time when object is created
138	  // - Overridden when [[suggestSeed]] or [[autoSeed]] is called
139	  // - Note that suggestSeed does *not* prevent autoSeed from changing this
140	  private var naming_prefix: Prefix = Builder.getPrefix
141	
142	  /** Takes the last seed suggested. Multiple calls to this function will take the last given seed, unless
143	    * this HasId is a module port (see overridden method in Data.scala).
144	    *
145	    * If the final computed name conflicts with the final name of another signal, the final name may get uniquified by
146	    * appending a digit at the end of the name.
147	    *
148	    * Is a lower priority than [[suggestName]], in that regardless of whether [[autoSeed]]
149	    * was called, [[suggestName]] will always take precedence if it was called.
150	    *
151	    * @param seed Seed for the name of this component
152	    * @return this object
153	    */
154	  private[chisel3] def autoSeed(seed: String): this.type = forceAutoSeed(seed)
155	  // Bypass the overridden behavior of autoSeed in [[Data]], apply autoSeed even to ports
156	  private[chisel3] def forceAutoSeed(seed: String): this.type = {
157	    if (!_nameIsSuggested) {
158	      suggested_seedVar = seed
159	    }
160	    naming_prefix = Builder.getPrefix
161	    this
162	  }
163	
164	  // Private internal version of suggestName that tells you if the name changed
165	  // Returns Some(old name, old prefix) if name changed, None otherwise
166	  private[chisel3] def _suggestNameCheck(seed: => String): Option[(String, Prefix)] = {
167	    val oldSeed = this.seedOpt
168	    val oldPrefix = this.naming_prefix
169	    suggestName(seed)
170	    if (oldSeed.nonEmpty && (oldSeed != this.seedOpt || oldPrefix != this.naming_prefix)) {
171	      Some(oldSeed.get -> oldPrefix)
172	    } else None
173	  }
174	
175	  /** Takes the first seed suggested. Multiple calls to this function will be ignored.
176	    * If the final computed name conflicts with another name, it may get uniquified by appending
177	    * a digit at the end.
178	    *
179	    * Is a higher priority than `autoSeed`, in that regardless of whether `autoSeed`
180	    * was called, [[suggestName]] will always take precedence.
181	    *
182	    * @param seed The seed for the name of this component
183	    * @return this object
184	    */
185	  def suggestName(seed: => String): this.type = {
186	    if (!_nameIsSuggested) {
187	      suggested_seedVar = seed
188	      // Only set the prefix if a seed hasn't been suggested
189	      naming_prefix = Builder.getPrefix
190	      _nameIsSuggested = true
191	    }
192	    this
193	  }
194	
195	  /** Computes the name of this HasId, if one exists
196	    * @param defaultSeed Optionally provide default seed for computing the name
197	    * @return the name, if it can be computed
198	    */
199	  private[chisel3] def _computeName(defaultSeed: Option[String]): Option[String] = {
200	    seedOpt
201	      .orElse(defaultSeed)
202	      .map(name => buildName(name, naming_prefix.reverse))
203	  }
204	
205	  /** This resolves the precedence of [[autoSeed]] and [[suggestName]]
206	    *
207	    * @return the current calculation of a name, if it exists
208	    */
209	  private[chisel3] def seedOpt: Option[String] = suggested_seed
210	
211	  /** @return Whether either autoName or suggestName has been called */
212	  def hasSeed: Boolean = seedOpt.isDefined
213	
214	  // Uses a namespace to convert suggestion into a true name
215	  // Will not do any naming if the reference already assigned.
216	  // (e.g. tried to suggest a name to part of a Record)
217	  private[chisel3] def _forceName(
218	    default:    => String,
219	    namespace:  Namespace,
220	    errorIfDup: Boolean = false,
221	    refBuilder: String => Arg = Ref(_)
222	  ): Unit =
223	    if (_ref.isEmpty) {
224	      val candidate_name = _computeName(Some(default).filterNot(_ => errorIfDup)).getOrElse {
225	        throwException(
226	          s"Attempted to name a nameless IO port ($this): this is usually caused by instantiating an IO but not assigning it to a val.\n" +
227	            s"Assign $this to a val, or explicitly call suggestName to seed a unique name"
228	        )
229	      }
230	
231	      val sanitized = sanitize(candidate_name)
232	      val available_name = namespace.name(candidate_name)
233	
234	      // Check for both cases of name duplication
235	      if (errorIfDup && (available_name != sanitized)) {
236	        // If sanitization occurred, then the sanitized name duplicate an existing name
237	        if ((candidate_name != sanitized)) {
238	          Builder.error(
239	            s"Attempted to name $this with an unsanitary name '$candidate_name': sanitization results in a duplicated name '$sanitized'. Please seed a more unique name"
240	          )(UnlocatableSourceInfo)
241	        } else {
242	          // Otherwise the candidate name duplicates an existing name
243	          Builder.error(
244	            s"Attempted to name $this with a duplicated name '$candidate_name'. Use suggestName to seed a unique name"
245	          )(UnlocatableSourceInfo)
246	        }
247	      }
248	
249	      setRef(refBuilder(available_name))
250	      // Clear naming prefix to free memory
251	      naming_prefix = Nil
252	    }
253	
254	  private var _refVar:                   Arg = null // using nullable var for better memory usage
255	  private def _ref:                      Option[Arg] = Option(_refVar)
256	  private[chisel3] def setRef(imm: Arg): Unit = setRef(imm, false)
257	  private[chisel3] def setRef(imm: Arg, force: Boolean): Unit = {
258	    if (_ref.isEmpty || force) {
259	      _refVar = imm
260	    }
261	  }
262	  private[chisel3] def setRef(parent: Node, name: String, opaque: Boolean = false): Unit = {
263	    if (!opaque) setRef(Slot(parent, name))
264	    else setRef(OpaqueSlot(parent))
265	  }
266	
267	  private[chisel3] def setRef(parent: Node, index: Int): Unit = setRef(LitIndex(parent, index))
268	  private[chisel3] def setRef(parent: Node, index: UInt): Unit = index.litOption match {
269	    case Some(lit) if lit.isValidInt => setRef(LitIndex(parent, lit.intValue))
270	    case _                           => setRef(Index(parent, index.ref(UnlocatableSourceInfo)))
271	  }
272	  private[chisel3] def getRef:       Arg = _ref.get
273	  private[chisel3] def getOptionRef: Option[Arg] = _ref
274	
275	  private def refName(c: Component): String = _ref match {
276	    case Some(arg) => arg.fullName(c)
277	    case None => {
278	      throwException(
279	        "You cannot access the .instanceName or .toTarget of non-hardware Data" + _errorContext
280	      )
281	    }
282	  }
283	
284	  private[chisel3] def _errorContext: String = {
285	    val nameGuess: String = _computeName(None) match {
286	      case Some(name) => s": '$name'"
287	      case None       => ""
288	    }
289	
290	    val parentGuess: String = _parent match {
291	      case Some(ViewParent) => s", in module '${reifyParent.pathName}'"
292	      case Some(p)          => s", in module '${p.pathName}'"
293	      case None             => ""
294	    }
295	
296	    nameGuess + parentGuess
297	  }
298	
299	  /** Helper for reifying views if they map to a single Target.
300	    *
301	    * This ignores writability, use chisel3.experimental.dataview.reify for non-Target use cases.
302	    */
303	  private[chisel3] def reifyTarget: Option[Data] = this match {
304	    case d: Data => reifySingleTarget(d) // Only Data can be views
305	    case bad => throwException(s"This shouldn't be possible - got $bad with ${_parent}")
306	  }
307	
308	  // Helper for reifying the parent of a view if the view maps to a single Target
309	  private[chisel3] def reifyParent: BaseModule = reifyTarget.flatMap(_._parent).getOrElse(ViewParent)
310	
311	  // Implementation of public methods.
312	  def instanceName: String = _parent match {
313	    case Some(ViewParent) => reifyTarget.map(_.instanceName).getOrElse(this.refName(ViewParent.fakeComponent))
314	    case Some(p) =>
315	      (p._component, this) match {
316	        case (Some(c), _)                                                   => refName(c)
317	        case (None, d: Data) if d.topBindingOpt == Some(CrossModuleBinding) => _ref.get.localName
318	        case (None, _: MemBase[_])                                          => _ref.get.localName
319	        case (None, _) if _ref.isDefined => {
320	          // Support instance names for HasIds that don't have a _parent set yet, but do have a _ref set.
321	          // This allows HasIds to be named in atModuleBodyEnd, for example.
322	          // In this case, we directly use the localName. This is valid, because the only time names are
323	          // context-dependent is on ports. If a port doesn't have a _parent set yet, the port must be within
324	          // the currently elaborating module, and should be named by its localName.
325	          _ref.get.localName
326	        }
327	        case (None, _) =>
328	          throwException(s"signalName/pathName should be called after circuit elaboration: $this, ${_parent}")
329	      }
330	    case None => throwException("this cannot happen")
331	  }
332	  def pathName: String = _parent match {
333	    case None             => instanceName
334	    case Some(ViewParent) => s"${reifyParent.pathName}.$instanceName"
335	    case Some(p)          => s"${p.pathName}.$instanceName"
336	  }
337	  def parentPathName: String = _parent match {
338	    case Some(ViewParent) => reifyParent.pathName
339	    case Some(p)          => p.pathName
340	    case None             => throwException(s"$instanceName doesn't have a parent")
341	  }
342	  def parentModName: String = _parent match {
343	    case Some(ViewParent) => reifyParent.name
344	    case Some(p)          => p.name
345	    case None             => throwException(s"$instanceName doesn't have a parent")
346	  }
347	}
348	
349	/** Holds the implementation of toNamed for Data and MemBase */
350	private[chisel3] trait NamedComponent extends HasId {
351	
352	  /** Returns a FIRRTL ComponentName that references this object
353	    * @note Should not be called until circuit elaboration is complete
354	    */
355	  final def toNamed: ComponentName = {
356	    assertValidTarget()
357	    ComponentName(this.instanceName, ModuleName(this.parentModName))
358	  }
359	
360	  /** Returns a FIRRTL ReferenceTarget that references this object
361	    * @note Should not be called until circuit elaboration is complete
362	    */
363	  final def toTarget: ReferenceTarget = {
364	    assertValidTarget()
365	    val name = this.instanceName
366	    if (!validComponentName(name)) throwException(s"Illegal component name: $name (note: literals are illegal)")
367	    import _root_.firrtl.annotations.{Target, TargetToken}
368	    val root = _parent.map {
369	      case ViewParent => reifyParent
370	      case other      => other
371	    }.get.getTarget // All NamedComponents will have a parent, only the top module can have None here
372	    Target.toTargetTokens(name).toList match {
373	      case TargetToken.Ref(r) :: components => root.ref(r).copy(component = components)
374	      case other =>
375	        throw _root_.firrtl.annotations.Target.NamedException(s"Cannot convert $name into [[ReferenceTarget]]: $other")
376	    }
377	  }
378	
379	  final def toAbsoluteTarget: ReferenceTarget = {
380	    val localTarget = toTarget
381	    def makeTarget(p: BaseModule) = p.toAbsoluteTarget.ref(localTarget.ref).copy(component = localTarget.component)
382	    _parent match {
383	      case Some(ViewParent) => makeTarget(reifyParent)
384	      case Some(parent)     => makeTarget(parent)
385	      case None             => localTarget
386	    }
387	  }
388	
389	  /** Returns a FIRRTL ReferenceTarget that references this object, relative to an optional root.
390	    *
391	    * If `root` is defined, the target is a hierarchical path starting from `root`.
392	    *
393	    * If `root` is not defined, the target is a hierarchical path equivalent to `toAbsoluteTarget`.
394	    *
395	    * @note If `root` is defined, and has not finished elaboration, this must be called within `atModuleBodyEnd`.
396	    * @note The NamedComponent must be a descendant of `root`, if it is defined.
397	    * @note This doesn't have special handling for Views.
398	    */
399	  final def toRelativeTarget(root: Option[BaseModule]): ReferenceTarget = {
400	    val localTarget = toTarget
401	    def makeTarget(p: BaseModule) =
402	      p.toRelativeTarget(root).ref(localTarget.ref).copy(component = localTarget.component)
403	    _parent match {
404	      case Some(ViewParent) => makeTarget(reifyParent)
405	      case Some(parent)     => makeTarget(parent)
406	      case None             => localTarget
407	    }
408	  }
409	
410	  /** Returns a FIRRTL ReferenceTarget that references this object, relative to an optional root.
411	    *
412	    * If `root` is defined, the target is a hierarchical path starting from `root`.
413	    *
414	    * If `root` is not defined, the target is a hierarchical path equivalent to `toAbsoluteTarget`.
415	    *
416	    * @note If `root` is defined, and has not finished elaboration, this must be called within `atModuleBodyEnd`.
417	    * @note The NamedComponent must be a descendant of `root`, if it is defined.
418	    * @note This doesn't have special handling for Views.
419	    */
420	  final def toRelativeTargetToHierarchy(root: Option[Hierarchy[BaseModule]]): ReferenceTarget = {
421	    val localTarget = toTarget
422	    def makeTarget(p: BaseModule) =
423	      p.toRelativeTargetToHierarchy(root).ref(localTarget.ref).copy(component = localTarget.component)
424	    _parent match {
425	      case Some(ViewParent) => makeTarget(reifyParent)
426	      case Some(parent)     => makeTarget(parent)
427	      case None             => localTarget
428	    }
429	  }
430	
431	  private def assertValidTarget(): Unit = {
432	    val isVecSubaccess = getOptionRef.map {
433	      case Index(_, _: ULit) => true // Vec literal indexing
434	      case Index(_, _: Node) => true // Vec dynamic indexing
435	      case _                 => false
436	    }.getOrElse(false)
437	
438	    if (isVecSubaccess) {
439	      throwException(
440	        s"You cannot target Vec subaccess" + _errorContext +
441	          ". Instead, assign it to a temporary (for example, with WireInit) and target the temporary."
442	      )
443	    }
444	  }
445	}
446	
447	// Mutable global state for chisel that can appear outside a Builder context
448	private[chisel3] class ChiselContext() {
449	  val modulePrefixSeperator: String = "_"
450	  val idGen = new IdGen
451	
452	  // Records the different prefixes which have been scoped at this point in time
453	  var prefixStack: Prefix = Nil
454	
455	  // Views belong to a separate namespace (for renaming)
456	  // The namespace outside of Builder context is useless, but it ensures that views can still be created
457	  // and the resulting .toTarget is very clearly useless (_$$View$$_...)
458	  val viewNamespace = Namespace.empty
459	
460	  // Pairs of Prefix and whether to include the separator
461	  var modulePrefixStack: List[(String, Boolean)] = Nil
462	}
463	
464	@nowarn("msg=Avoid custom annotations")
465	private[chisel3] class DynamicContext(
466	  val annotationSeq:       AnnotationSeq,
467	  val throwOnFirstError:   Boolean,
468	  val useLegacyWidth:      Boolean,
469	  val includeUtilMetadata: Boolean,
470	  val useSRAMBlackbox:     Boolean,
471	  val warningFilters:      Seq[WarningFilter],
472	  val sourceRoots:         Seq[File],
473	  val defaultNamespace:    Option[Namespace],
474	  // Definitions from other scopes in the same elaboration, use allDefinitions below
475	  val loggerOptions:      LoggerOptions,
476	  val definitions:        mutable.LinkedHashSet[Definition[_ <: BaseModule]],
477	  val contextCache:       BuilderContextCache,
478	  val layerMap:           Map[layer.Layer, layer.Layer],
479	  val inlineTestIncluder: InlineTestIncluder,
480	  val suppressSourceInfo: Boolean,
481	  var elideLayerBlocks:   Boolean,
482	  val elaborationTrace:   ElaborationTrace
483	) {
484	  val importedDefinitionAnnos = annotationSeq.collect { case a: ImportDefinitionAnnotation[_] => a }
485	
486	  // Map from proto module name to ext-module name
487	  // Pick the definition name by default in case not overridden
488	  // 1. Ensure there are no repeated names for imported Definitions - both Proto Names as well as ExtMod Names
489	  // 2. Return the distinct definition / extMod names
490	  val importedDefinitionMap = importedDefinitionAnnos
491	    .map(a => a.definition.proto.name -> a.overrideDefName.getOrElse(a.definition.proto.name))
492	    .toMap
493	
494	  private def checkAndGetDistinctProtoExtModNames() = {
495	    val allProtoNames = importedDefinitionAnnos.map { a => a.definition.proto.name }
496	    val distinctProtoNames = importedDefinitionMap.keys.toSeq
497	    val allExtModNames = importedDefinitionMap.toSeq.map(_._2)
498	    val distinctExtModNames = allExtModNames.distinct
499	
500	    if (distinctProtoNames.length < allProtoNames.length) {
501	      val duplicates = allProtoNames.diff(distinctProtoNames).mkString(", ")
502	      throwException(s"Expected distinct imported Definition names but found duplicates for: $duplicates")
503	    }
504	    if (distinctExtModNames.length < allExtModNames.length) {
505	      val duplicates = allExtModNames.diff(distinctExtModNames).mkString(", ")
506	      throwException(s"Expected distinct overrideDef names but found duplicates for: $duplicates")
507	    }
508	    (
509	      (allProtoNames ++ allExtModNames).distinct,
510	      importedDefinitionAnnos.map(a => a.definition.proto.definitionIdentifier)
511	    )
512	  }
513	
514	  val globalNamespace = defaultNamespace.getOrElse(Namespace.empty)
515	  val globalIdentifierNamespace = Namespace.empty('$')
516	
517	  // A mapping from previously named bundles to their hashed structural/FIRRTL types, for
518	  // disambiguation purposes when emitting type aliases
519	  // Records are used as the key for this map to both represent their alias name and preserve
520	  // the chisel Bundle structure when passing everything off to the Converter
521	  private[chisel3] val aliasMap: mutable.LinkedHashMap[String, (fir.Type, SourceInfo)] =
522	    mutable.LinkedHashMap.empty[String, (fir.Type, SourceInfo)]
523	
524	  // Ensure imported Definitions emit as ExtModules with the correct name so
525	  // that instantiations will also use the correct name and prevent any name
526	  // conflicts with Modules/Definitions in this elaboration
527	  checkAndGetDistinctProtoExtModNames() match {
528	    case (names, identifiers) =>
529	      names.foreach(globalNamespace.name(_))
530	      identifiers.foreach(globalIdentifierNamespace.name(_))
531	  }
532	
533	  val components = ArrayBuffer[Component]()
534	  val annotations = ArrayBuffer[() => Seq[Annotation]]()
535	  val layers = mutable.LinkedHashSet[layer.Layer]()
536	  val options = mutable.LinkedHashSet[choice.Case]()
537	  val domains = mutable.LinkedHashSet[domain.Domain]()
538	  var currentModule: Option[BaseModule] = None
539	
540	  // Views that do not correspond to a single ReferenceTarget and thus require renaming
541	  val unnamedViews: ArrayBuffer[Data] = ArrayBuffer.empty
542	
543	  // Set by object Module.apply before calling class Module constructor
544	  // Used to distinguish between no Module() wrapping, multiple wrappings, and rewrapping
545	  var readyForModuleConstr: Boolean = false
546	  var whenStack:            List[WhenContext] = Nil
547	  var blockStack:           List[Block] = Nil
548	  // Clock and Reset are "Delayed" because ImplicitClock and ImplicitReset need to set these values,
549	  // But the clock or reset defined by the user won't yet be initialized
550	  var currentClock:   Option[Delayed[Clock]] = None
551	  var currentReset:   Option[Delayed[Reset]] = None
552	  var currentDisable: Disable.Type = Disable.BeforeReset
553	  var enabledLayers:  mutable.LinkedHashSet[layer.Layer] = mutable.LinkedHashSet.empty
554	  var layerStack:     List[layer.Layer] = layer.Layer.root :: Nil
555	  val errors = new ErrorLog(warningFilters, sourceRoots, throwOnFirstError)
556	  val namingStack = new NamingStack
557	
558	  // Used to indicate if this is the top-level module of full elaboration, or from a Definition
559	  var inDefinition: Boolean = false
560	}
561	
562	@nowarn("msg=Avoid custom annotations")
563	private[chisel3] object Builder extends LazyLogging {
564	
565	  // Represents the current state of the prefixes given
566	  type Prefix = List[String]
567	
568	  // All global mutable state must be referenced via dynamicContextVar!!
569	  private val dynamicContextVar = new DynamicVariable[Option[DynamicContext]](None)
570	  private def dynamicContext: DynamicContext = {
571	    require(dynamicContextVar.value.isDefined, "must be inside Builder context")
572	    dynamicContextVar.value.get
573	  }
574	
575	  /** Check if we are in a Builder context */
576	  def inContext: Boolean = dynamicContextVar.value.isDefined
577	
578	  // Used to suppress warnings when casting from a UInt to an Enum
579	  var suppressEnumCastWarning: Boolean = false
580	
581	  // Returns the current dynamic context
582	  def captureContext(): DynamicContext = dynamicContext
583	
584	  // Ensure we have a thread-specific ChiselContext
585	  private val chiselContext = new ThreadLocal[ChiselContext] {
586	    override def initialValue: ChiselContext = {
587	      new ChiselContext
588	    }
589	  }
590	
591	  def namingStackOption: Option[NamingStack] = dynamicContextVar.value.map(_.namingStack)
592	
593	  def idGen: IdGen = chiselContext.get.idGen
594	
595	  def globalNamespace:           Namespace = dynamicContext.globalNamespace
596	  def globalIdentifierNamespace: Namespace = dynamicContext.globalIdentifierNamespace
597	
598	  def aliasMap: mutable.LinkedHashMap[String, (fir.Type, SourceInfo)] =
599	    dynamicContext.aliasMap
600	
601	  def components:  ArrayBuffer[Component] = dynamicContext.components
602	  def definitions: mutable.LinkedHashSet[Definition[_ <: BaseModule]] = dynamicContext.definitions
603	  def addDefinition(definition: Definition[_ <: BaseModule]): Unit = {
604	    // Only add a definition if it isn't an imported definition
605	    // LinkedHashSet naturally prevents duplicates
606	    if (!dynamicContext.importedDefinitionMap.contains(definition.proto.name)) {
607	      dynamicContext.definitions += definition
608	    }
609	  }
610	
611	  def annotations: ArrayBuffer[() => Seq[Annotation]] = dynamicContext.annotations
612	
613	  def layers:  mutable.LinkedHashSet[layer.Layer] = dynamicContext.layers
614	  def options: mutable.LinkedHashSet[choice.Case] = dynamicContext.options
615	  def domains: mutable.LinkedHashSet[domain.Domain] = dynamicContext.domains
616	
617	  def contextCache: BuilderContextCache = dynamicContext.contextCache
618	
619	  def annotationSeq:         AnnotationSeq = dynamicContext.annotationSeq
620	  def importedDefinitionMap: Map[String, String] = dynamicContext.importedDefinitionMap
621	
622	  def unnamedViews:  ArrayBuffer[Data] = dynamicContext.unnamedViews
623	  def viewNamespace: Namespace = chiselContext.get.viewNamespace
624	
625	  // Puts a prefix string onto the prefix stack
626	  def pushPrefix(d: String): Unit = {
627	    val context = chiselContext.get()
628	    context.prefixStack = d :: context.prefixStack
629	  }
630	
631	  /** Pushes the current name of a data onto the prefix stack
632	    *
633	    * @param d data to derive prefix from
634	    * @return whether anything was pushed to the stack
635	    */
636	  def pushPrefix(d: HasId): Boolean = {
637	    def buildAggName(id: HasId): Option[String] = {
638	      def getSubName(field: Data): Option[String] = field.getOptionRef.flatMap {
639	        case Slot(_, field)       => Some(field) // Record
640	        case OpaqueSlot(_)        => None // OpaqueSlots don't contribute to the name
641	        case Index(_, ILit(n))    => Some(n.toString) // Vec static indexing
642	        case LitIndex(_, n)       => Some(n.toString) // Vec static indexing
643	        case Index(_, ULit(n, _)) => Some(n.toString) // Vec lit indexing
644	        case Index(_, _: Node)    => None // Vec dynamic indexing
645	        case ModuleIO(_, n)       => Some(n) // BlackBox port
646	        case f =>
647	          throw new InternalErrorException(s"Match Error: field=$f")
648	      }
649	      def map2[A, B](a: Option[A], b: Option[A])(f: (A, A) => B): Option[B] =
650	        a.flatMap(ax => b.map(f(ax, _)))
651	      // If the binding is None, this is an illegal connection and later logic will error
652	      def recData(_data: Data): Option[String] = {
653	        val data = reifySingleTarget(_data).getOrElse(_data)
654	        data.binding.flatMap {
655	          case (_: WireBinding | _: RegBinding | _: MemoryPortBinding | _: OpBinding | _: InstanceChoiceBinding) =>
656	            data.seedOpt
657	          case ChildBinding(parent) =>
658	            recData(parent).map { p =>
659	              // And name of the field if we have one, we don't for dynamic indexing of Vecs
660	              getSubName(data).map(p + "_" + _).getOrElse(p)
661	            }
662	          case DynamicIndexBinding(vec)                                => recData(vec)
663	          case SampleElementBinding(parent)                            => recData(parent)
664	          case PortBinding(mod) if Builder.currentModule.contains(mod) => data.seedOpt
665	          case PortBinding(mod)                                        => map2(mod.seedOpt, data.seedOpt)(_ + "_" + _)
666	          case (_: LitBinding | _: DontCareBinding)                    => None
667	          case _                                                       => Some("view_") // TODO implement complex views.
668	        }
669	      }
670	      id match {
671	        case d: Data => recData(d)
672	        case _ => None
673	      }
674	    }
675	    buildAggName(d).map { name =>
676	      if (isTemp(name)) {
677	        pushPrefix(name.tail)
678	      } else {
679	        pushPrefix(name)
680	      }
681	    }.isDefined
682	  }
683	
684	  // Remove a prefix from top of the stack
685	  def popPrefix(): List[String] = {
686	    val context = chiselContext.get()
687	    val tail = context.prefixStack.tail
688	    context.prefixStack = tail
689	    tail
690	  }
691	
692	  // Removes all prefixes from the prefix stack
693	  def clearPrefix(): Unit = {
694	    chiselContext.get().prefixStack = Nil
695	  }
696	
697	  // Clears existing prefixes and sets to new prefix stack
698	  def setPrefix(prefix: Prefix): Unit = {
699	    chiselContext.get().prefixStack = prefix
700	  }
701	
702	  // Returns the prefix stack at this moment
703	  def getPrefix: Prefix = chiselContext.get().prefixStack
704	
705	  def currentModule: Option[BaseModule] = dynamicContextVar.value match {
706	    case Some(dynamicContext) => dynamicContext.currentModule
707	    case _                    => None
708	  }
709	  def currentModule_=(target: Option[BaseModule]): Unit = {
710	    dynamicContext.currentModule = target
711	  }
712	
713	  /** Retrieves the parent of a module based on the elaboration context
714	    *
715	    * @param module the module to get the parent of
716	    * @param context the context the parent should be evaluated in
717	    * @return the parent of the module provided
718	    */
719	  def retrieveParent(module: BaseModule, context: BaseModule): Option[BaseModule] = {
720	    module._parent
721	  }
722	  def referenceUserModule: RawModule = {
723	    currentModule match {
724	      case Some(module: RawModule) => module
725	      case _ =>
726	        throwException(
727	          "Error: Not in a RawModule. Likely cause: Missed Module() wrap, bare chisel API call, or attempting to construct hardware inside a BlackBox."
728	          // A bare api call is, e.g. calling Wire() from the scala console).
729	        )
730	    }
731	  }
732	  def referenceUserContainer: BaseModule = {
733	    currentModule match {
734	      case Some(module: RawModule) => module
735	      case Some(cls: Class)        => cls
736	      case _ =>
737	        throwException(
738	          "Error: Not in a RawModule or Class. Likely cause: Missed Module() or Definition() wrap, bare chisel API call, or attempting to construct hardware inside a BlackBox."
739	          // A bare api call is, e.g. calling Wire() from the scala console).
740	        )
741	    }
742	  }
743	  def forcedUserModule: RawModule = currentModule match {
744	    case Some(module: RawModule) => module
745	    case _ =>
746	      throwException(
747	        "Error: Not in a UserModule. Likely cause: Missed Module() wrap, bare chisel API call, or attempting to construct hardware inside a BlackBox."
748	        // A bare api call is, e.g. calling Wire() from the scala console).
749	      )
750	  }
751	  def hasDynamicContext: Boolean = dynamicContextVar.value.isDefined
752	
753	  def readyForModuleConstr: Boolean = dynamicContext.readyForModuleConstr
754	  def readyForModuleConstr_=(target: Boolean): Unit = {
755	    dynamicContext.readyForModuleConstr = target
756	  }
757	
758	  def whenDepth: Int = dynamicContext.whenStack.length
759	
760	  def pushWhen(wc: WhenContext): Unit = {
761	    dynamicContext.whenStack = wc :: dynamicContext.whenStack
762	  }
763	
764	  def popWhen(): WhenContext = {
765	    val lastWhen = dynamicContext.whenStack.head
766	    dynamicContext.whenStack = dynamicContext.whenStack.tail
767	    lastWhen
768	  }
769	
770	  def whenStack: List[WhenContext] = dynamicContext.whenStack
771	  def whenStack_=(s: List[WhenContext]): Unit = {
772	    dynamicContext.whenStack = s
773	  }
774	
775	  def blockStack: List[Block] = dynamicContext.blockStack
776	  def blockStack_=(s: List[Block]): Unit = {
777	    dynamicContext.blockStack = s
778	  }
779	
780	  def blockDepth: Int = dynamicContext.blockStack.length
781	  def pushBlock(b: Block): Unit = {
782	    dynamicContext.blockStack = b :: dynamicContext.blockStack
783	  }
784	
785	  def popBlock(): Block = {
786	    val lastBlock = dynamicContext.blockStack.head
787	    dynamicContext.blockStack = dynamicContext.blockStack.tail
788	    lastBlock
789	  }
790	
791	  def currentBlock: Option[Block] = dynamicContextVar.value match {
792	    case Some(dc) => dc.blockStack.headOption
793	    case _        => None
794	  }
795	
796	  // Helper for reasonable errors when clock or reset value not yet initialized
797	  private def getDelayed[A](field: String, dc: Delayed[A]): A = {
798	    // TODO add SourceInfo and change to Builder.exception.
799	    def err(extra: String) = {
800	      throwException(
801	        s"The implicit $field is null which means the code that sets its definition has not yet executed. $extra."
802	      )
803	    }
804	    val result =
805	      try {
806	        dc.value
807	      } catch {
808	        // If user uses -Xcheckinit there will be additional information.
809	        case UninitializedFieldError(msg) => err(msg)
810	      }
811	    // If user is not using -Xcheckinit, suggest that they do.
812	    if (result == null) {
813	      err("Try adding -Xcheckinit to your scalacOptions to get a more useful stack trace")
814	    }
815	    result
816	  }
817	
818	  /** Safely get the current Clock for use */
819	  def currentClock: Option[Clock] =
820	    dynamicContext.currentClock.map(d => getDelayed("clock", d))
821	
822	  /** Get the underlying box around current Clock, only used for saving the value */
823	  def currentClockDelayed: Option[Delayed[Clock]] = dynamicContext.currentClock
824	  def currentClock_=(newClock: Option[Delayed[Clock]]): Unit = {
825	    dynamicContext.currentClock = newClock
826	  }
827	
828	  /** Safely get the current Reset for use */
829	  def currentReset: Option[Reset] =
830	    dynamicContext.currentReset.map(d => getDelayed("reset", d))
831	
832	  /** Get the underlying box around current Reset, only used for saving the value */
833	  def currentResetDelayed: Option[Delayed[Reset]] = dynamicContext.currentReset
834	  def currentReset_=(newReset: Option[Delayed[Reset]]): Unit = {

868	  private def validateGroupCases(
869	    groupName:     String,
870	    expectedCases: Seq[String],
871	    actualCases:   Seq[String],
872	    context:       String = ""
873	  ): Unit = {
874	    if (expectedCases != actualCases) {
875	      val contextMsg = if (context.nonEmpty) s" ($context)" else ""
876	      throw new IllegalArgumentException(
877	        s"Group '$groupName' has inconsistent case definitions$contextMsg.\n" +
878	          s"  Expected cases: ${expectedCases.mkString(", ")}\n" +
879	          s"  Found cases: ${actualCases.mkString(", ")}"
880	      )
881	    }
882	  }

893	  // TODO(twigg): Ideally, binding checks and new bindings would all occur here
894	  // However, rest of frontend can't support this yet.
895	  def pushCommand[T <: Command](c: T): T = {
896	    forcedUserModule.addCommand(c)
897	    c
898	  }
899	  def pushOp[T <: Data](cmd: DefPrim[T]): T = {
900	    // Bind each element of the returned Data to being a Op
901	    cmd.id.bind(OpBinding(forcedUserModule, currentBlock))

905	  /** Recursively suggests names to supported "container" classes
906	    * Arbitrary nestings of supported classes are allowed so long as the
907	    * innermost element is of type HasId
908	    * (Note: Map is Iterable[Tuple2[_,_]] and thus excluded)
909	    */
910	  def nameRecursively(prefix: String, nameMe: Any, namer: (HasId, String) => Unit): Unit = nameMe match {
911	    case (id: Instance[_]) =>
912	      id.underlying match {
913	        case Clone(m: experimental.hierarchy.ModuleClone[_]) => namer(m.getPorts, prefix)
914	        case _                                               =>
915	      }
916	    case (d: Data) =>
917	      // Views are often returned in lieu of the target, so name the target (as appropriate).
918	      // If a view but not identity, return the view and name it since it shows up in .toString and error messages.
919	      // TODO recurse on targets of non-identity views, perhaps with additional prefix from the view.
920	      val reified = reifyIdentityView(d).fold(d)(_._1)
921	      namer(reified, prefix)
922	    case (id: HasId) => namer(id, prefix)
923	    case Some(elt) => nameRecursively(prefix, elt, namer)
924	    case (iter: Iterable[_]) if iter.hasDefiniteSize =>
925	      for ((elt, i) <- iter.zipWithIndex) {
926	        nameRecursively(s"${prefix}_${i}", elt, namer)
927	      }
928	    case product: Product =>
929	      product.productIterator.zip(product.productElementNames).foreach { case (elt, fullName) =>
930	        val name = fullName.stripPrefix("_")
931	        val prefixedName = if (name.nonEmpty) s"${prefix}_${name}" else prefix
932	        nameRecursively(prefixedName, elt, namer)
933	      }
934	    case disable: Disable => nameRecursively(prefix, disable.value, namer)
935	    case _ => // Do nothing
936	  }
937

939	  def error(m: => String)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit = {
940	    // If --throw-on-first-error is requested, throw an exception instead of aggregating errors
941	    if (dynamicContextVar.value.isDefined) {
942	      errors.error(m, sourceInfo)
943	    } else {
944	      throwException(m)
945	    }
946	  }
947	  def warning(warning: Warning): Unit =
948	    if (dynamicContextVar.value.isDefined) errors.warning(warning)
949

951	    if (dynamicContextVar.value.isDefined) errors.deprecated(m, location)

953	  /** Record an exception as an error, and throw it.
954	    *
955	    * @param m exception message
956	    */
957	  @throws(classOf[chisel3.ChiselException])
958	  def exception(m: => String)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Nothing = {
959	    error(m)
960	    throwException(m)

969	  // Builds a RenameMap for all Views that do not correspond to a single Data
970	  // These Data give a fake ReferenceTarget for .toTarget and .toReferenceTarget that the returned
971	  // RenameMap can split into the constituent parts
972	  private[chisel3] def makeViewRenameMap(circuitName: String): MutableRenameMap = {
973	    val renames = MutableRenameMap(circuitName)
974	    for (view <- unnamedViews if !view.isLit) { // Aggregates can be literals too!
975	      reifySingleTarget(view) match {
976	        // If the target reifies to a single target, we don't need to rename.
977	        // This is ludicrously expensive though, find a way to make this code unnecessary.
978	        case Some(_) => ()
979	        case None =>
980	          val localTarget = view.toTarget
981	          val absTarget = view.toAbsoluteTarget
982	          val elts = getRecursiveFields.lazily(view, "").collect { case (elt: Element, _) => elt }
983	          for (elt <- elts if !elt.isLit) {
984	            // This is a hack to not crash when .viewAs is called on non-hardware
985	            // It can be removed in Chisel 6.0.0 when it becomes illegal to call .viewAs on non-hardware
986	            val targetOfViewOpt =
987	              try {
988	                Some(reify(elt)._1) // Writability is irrelevant here
989	              } catch {
990	                case _: NoSuchElementException => None
991	              }
992	            // It is legal to target DontCare, but we obviously cannot rename to DontCare
993	            targetOfViewOpt
994	              .filterNot(_.binding.contains(DontCareBinding()))
995	              .foreach { targetOfView =>
996	                renames.record(localTarget, targetOfView.toTarget)
997	                renames.record(absTarget, targetOfView.toAbsoluteTarget)
998	              }
999	          }
1000	      }
1001	    }
1002	    renames
1003	  }

1005	  def setRecordAlias(record: Record with HasTypeAlias, parentDirection: SpecifiedDirection): Unit = {
1006	    val finalizedAlias: Option[String] = {
1007	      val sourceInfo = record.aliasName.info
1008	
1009	      // If the aliased bundle is coerced and it has flipped signals, then they must be stripped
1010	      val isCoerced = parentDirection match {
1011	        case SpecifiedDirection.Input | SpecifiedDirection.Output => true
1012	        case other                                                => false
1013	      }
1014	      val isStripped = isCoerced && record.containsAFlipped
1015	
1016	      // The true alias, after sanitization and (TODO) disambiguation
1017	      val alias = sanitize(s"${record.aliasName.id}${if (isStripped) record.aliasName.strippedSuffix else ""}")
1018	      // Filter out (TODO: disambiguate) FIRRTL keywords that cause parser errors if used
1019	      if (illegalTypeAliases.contains(alias)) {
1020	        Builder.error(
1021	          s"Attempted to use an illegal word '$alias' for a type alias. Chisel does not automatically disambiguate these aliases at this time."
1022	        )(sourceInfo)
1023	
1024	        None
1025	      } else {
1026	        val tpe = Converter.extractType(record, isCoerced, sourceInfo, true, true, aliasMap.keys.toSeq)
1027	        // If the name is already taken, check if there exists a *structurally equivalent* bundle with the same name, and
1028	        // simply error (TODO: disambiguate that name)
1029	        if (
1030	          Builder.aliasMap.contains(alias) &&
1031	          Builder.aliasMap.get(alias).exists(_._1 != tpe)
1032	        ) {
1033	          // Get full structural map value
1034	          val recordValue = Builder.aliasMap.get(alias).get
1035	          // Conflict found:
1036	          error(
1037	            s"Attempted to redeclare an existing type alias '$alias' with a new Record structure:\n'$tpe'.\n\nThe alias was previously defined as:\n'${recordValue._1}${recordValue._2
1038	                .makeMessage(" " + _)}"
1039	          )(sourceInfo)
1040	
1041	          None
1042	        } else {
1043	          if (!Builder.aliasMap.contains(alias)) {
1044	            Builder.aliasMap.put(alias, (tpe, sourceInfo))
1045	          }
1046	
1047	          Some(alias)
1048	        }
1049	      }
1050	    }
1051	    record.finalizedAlias = finalizedAlias
1052	  }

1054	  private[chisel3] def build[T <: BaseModule](
1055	    f:              => T,
1056	    dynamicContext: DynamicContext
1057	  ): (ElaboratedCircuit, T) = {
1058	    // Logger has its own context separate from Chisel's dynamic context
1059	    _root_.logger.Logger.makeScope(dynamicContext.loggerOptions) {
1060	      buildImpl(f, dynamicContext)
1061	    }
1062	  }
1063	
1064	  private def buildImpl[T <: BaseModule](
1065	    f:              => T,
1066	    dynamicContext: DynamicContext
1067	  ): (ElaboratedCircuit, T) = {
1068	    dynamicContextVar.withValue(Some(dynamicContext)) {
1069	      // Must initialize the singleton in a Builder context or weird things can happen
1070	      // in tiny designs/testcases that never access anything in chisel3.internal.
1071	      ViewParent: Unit
1072	      // Must initialize the singleton or OutOfMemoryErrors and StackOverflowErrors will instead report as
1073	      // "java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: Could not initialize class scala.util.control.NonFatal$".
1074	      scala.util.control.NonFatal: Unit
1075	      logger.info("Elaborating design...")
1076	      // Register all layers.  This will ensure that the FIRRTL produce always includes these layers.
1077	      chisel3.layers.defaultLayers.foreach(layer.addLayer)
1078	      val mod =
1079	        try {
1080	          val m = f
1081	          if (!inDefinition) { // This avoids definition name index skipping with D/I
1082	            m._forceName(m.name, globalNamespace)
1083	          }
1084	          m
1085	        } catch {
1086	          case NonFatal(e) =>
1087	            // Make sure to report any aggregated errors in case the Exception is due to a tainted return value
1088	            // Note that errors.checkpoint may throw an Exception which will suppress e
1089	            errors.checkpoint(logger)
1090	            throw e
1091	        }
1092	      errors.checkpoint(logger)
1093	      logger.info("Done elaborating.")
1094	
1095	      val typeAliases = aliasMap.flatMap { case (name, (underlying: fir.Type, info: SourceInfo)) =>
1096	        Some(DefTypeAlias(info, underlying, name))
1097	      }.toSeq
1098	
1099	      /** Stores an adjacency list representation of layers.  Connections indicating children. */
1100	      val layerAdjacencyList = mutable
1101	        .LinkedHashMap[layer.Layer, mutable.LinkedHashSet[layer.Layer]]()
1102	        .withDefault(_ => mutable.LinkedHashSet[layer.Layer]())
1103	
1104	      // Populate the adjacency list.
1105	      layers.foreach { layer =>
1106	        layerAdjacencyList(layer.parent) = layerAdjacencyList(layer.parent) += layer
1107	      }
1108	
1109	      /** For a `layer.Layer` all its children and fold them into a `Layer`.  This
1110	        * "folding" creates one `Layer` for each child nested under each parent
1111	        * `Layer`.
1112	        */
1113	      def foldLayers(l: layer.Layer): Layer = {
1114	        val children = layerAdjacencyList(l)
1115	        def path2str(path: java.nio.file.Path): String = {
1116	          System.getProperty("os.name").toLowerCase match {
1117	            case os if os.contains("windows") => path.toString.replace("\\", "\\\\")
1118	            case _                            => path.toString
1119	          }
1120	        }
1121	        val config = l.config match {
1122	          case layer.LayerConfig.Extract(_) => LayerConfig.Extract(l.outputDir.map(path2str))
1123	          case layer.LayerConfig.Inline     => LayerConfig.Inline
1124	          case layer.LayerConfig.Root       => ???
1125	        }
1126	        Layer(l.sourceInfo, l.name, config, children.map(foldLayers).toSeq, l)
1127	      }
1128	
1129	      // Group by name to handle multiple Group objects with the same name (e.g., multiple DynamicGroup instances)
1130	      val optionDefs = groupByIntoSeq(options)(opt => opt.group.name).map { case (groupName, cases) =>
1131	        val representativeGroup = cases.head.group
1132	        val allCaseNames = cases.map(_.name).distinct
1133	
1134	        // Validate all Group objects with same name have same cases
1135	        // Catches: static vs DynamicGroup conflicts, or DynamicGroups with same name but different cases
1136	        val distinctGroups = cases.map(_.group).distinct
1137	        if (distinctGroups.size > 1) {
1138	          distinctGroups.tail.foreach { otherGroup =>
1139	            val otherCases = cases.filter(_.group == otherGroup).map(_.name).distinct
1140	            validateGroupCases(groupName, allCaseNames, otherCases, "group conflict")
1141	          }
1142	        }
1143	
1144	        DefOption(
1145	          representativeGroup.sourceInfo,
1146	          groupName,
1147	          cases.distinctBy(_.name).map(optCase => DefOptionCase(optCase.sourceInfo, optCase.name))
1148	        )
1149	      }
1150	
1151	      // Make the main module (the last component) public if this is not a
1152	      // Circuit created from a Definition.
1153	      components.last match {
1154	        case module: DefModule if !inDefinition =>
1155	          components.update(
1156	            components.size - 1, {
1157	              val newModule = module.copy(isPublic = true)
1158	              newModule
1159	            }
1160	          )
1161	        case _ =>
1162	      }
1163	
1164	      val circuit =
1165	        Circuit(
1166	          components.last.name,
1167	          components.toSeq,
1168	          annotations.toSeq,
1169	          makeViewRenameMap(circuitName = components.last.name),
1170	          typeAliases,
1171	          layerAdjacencyList(layer.Layer.Root).map(foldLayers).toSeq,
1172	          optionDefs,
1173	          dynamicContext.suppressSourceInfo,
1174	          domains.toSeq.map(d => Domain(d.sourceInfo, d.name, d.fields))
1175	        )
1176	      (ElaboratedCircuit(circuit, dynamicContext.annotationSeq.toSeq), mod)
1177	    }
1178	  }
1179	
1180	  // Puts a module prefix string onto the module prefix stack
1181	  def pushModulePrefix(prefix: String, includeSeparator: Boolean): Unit = {
1182	    val context = chiselContext.get()
1183	    context.modulePrefixStack = (prefix, includeSeparator) :: context.modulePrefixStack
1184	  }
1185

1188	    val context = chiselContext.get()
1189	    val tail = context.modulePrefixStack.tail
1190	    context.modulePrefixStack = tail
1191	  }

1193	  // Returns the current module prefix stack without modifying it
1194	  def getModulePrefixStack: List[(String, Boolean)] = chiselContext.get().modulePrefixStack
1195

1197	  def clearModulePrefixStack(): List[(String, Boolean)] = {
1198	    val context = chiselContext.get()
1199	    val old = context.modulePrefixStack
1200	    context.modulePrefixStack = Nil
1201	    old
1202	  }
1203

1205	  def setModulePrefixStack(stack: List[(String, Boolean)]): Unit = {
1206	    chiselContext.get().modulePrefixStack = stack
1207	  }
1208	
1209	  // Returns the nested module prefix at this moment
1210	  def getModulePrefix: String = {
1211	    val ctx = chiselContext.get()
1212	    val modulePrefixStack = ctx.modulePrefixStack
1213	    val sep = ctx.modulePrefixSeperator
1214	    modulePrefixStack.foldLeft("") { case (acc, (elt, useSep)) =>
1215	      val s = if (useSep) sep else ""
1216	      elt + s + acc

1218	  }
1219	
1220	  def getModulePrefixSeperator: String = {
1221	    chiselContext.get().modulePrefixSeperator
1222	  }
1223	
1224	  /** The representation of the state of the [[Builder]] at a current point in
1225	    * time.  This is intended to capture _enough_ information to insert hardware
1226	    * at another point in the circuit.
1227	    *
1228	    * @see [[chisel3.Placeholder]]
1229	    */
1230	  case class State(
1231	    currentModule: Option[BaseModule],
1232	    whenStack:     List[WhenContext],
1233	    blockStack:    List[Block],
1234	    layerStack:    List[layer.Layer],
1235	    prefix:        Prefix,
1236	    clock:         Option[Delayed[Clock]],
1237	    reset:         Option[Delayed[Reset]],
1238	    enabledLayers: List[layer.Layer]
1239	  )
1240	
1241	  object State {
1242	
1243	    /** Return a [[State]] suitable for doing module construction.
1244	      */
1245	    def default: State = State(
1246	      currentModule = Builder.currentModule,
1247	      whenStack = Nil,
1248	      blockStack = Builder.blockStack,
1249	      layerStack = layer.Layer.Root :: Nil,
1250	      prefix = Nil,
1251	      clock = None,
1252	      reset = None,
1253	      enabledLayers = Nil
1254	    )
1255	
1256	    /** Capture the current [[Builder]] state.
1257	      */
1258	    def save: State = {
1259	      State(
1260	        currentModule = Builder.currentModule,
1261	        whenStack = Builder.whenStack,
1262	        blockStack = Builder.blockStack,
1263	        layerStack = Builder.layerStack,
1264	        prefix = Builder.getPrefix,
1265	        clock = Builder.currentClockDelayed,
1266	        reset = Builder.currentResetDelayed,
1267	        enabledLayers = Builder.enabledLayers.toList
1268	      )
1269	    }
1270	
1271	    /** Set the [[Builder]] state to that provided by the parameter.
1272	      *
1273	      * @param state the state to set the [[Builder]] to
1274	      */
1275	    def restore(state: State) = {
1276	      Builder.currentModule = state.currentModule
1277	      Builder.whenStack = state.whenStack
1278	      Builder.blockStack = state.blockStack
1279	      Builder.layerStack = state.layerStack
1280	      Builder.setPrefix(state.prefix)
1281	      Builder.currentClock = state.clock
1282	      Builder.currentReset = state.reset
1283	      Builder.enabledLayers.clear()
1284	      Builder.enabledLayers ++= state.enabledLayers
1285	    }
1286	
1287	    /** Run the `thunk` with the context provided by `state`.  Save the [[Builder]]
1288	      * state before the thunk and restore it afterwards.
1289	      *
1290	      * @param state change the [[Builder]] to this state when running the thunk
1291	      * @param thunk some hardware to generate
1292	      * @return whatever the `thunk` returns
1293	      */
1294	    def guard[A](state: State)(thunk: => A): A = {
1295	      val old = save
1296	      restore(state)
1297	      val result = thunk
1298	      restore(old)
1299	      result
1300	    }

1302	  }

1314	      case None              => DummyNamer

1318	  def popReturnContext[T <: Any](prefixRef: T, until: NamingContextInterface): T = {
1319	    until match {
1320	      case DummyNamer =>
1321	        require(
1322	          Builder.namingStackOption.isEmpty,
1323	          "Builder context must remain stable throughout a chiselName-annotated function invocation"
1324	        )
1325	      case context: NamingContext =>
1326	        require(
1327	          Builder.namingStackOption.isDefined,
1328	          "Builder context must remain stable throughout a chiselName-annotated function invocation"
1329	        )
1330	        Builder.namingStackOption.get.popContext(prefixRef, context)
1331	    }
1332	    prefixRef
1333	  }
1334	
1335	  def length(): Int = Builder.namingStackOption.get.length()
1336	}

1339	private[chisel3] object castToInt {
1340	  def apply(x: BigInt, msg: String): Int = {
1341	    val res = x.toInt
1342	    require(x == res, s"$msg $x is too large to be represented as Int")
1343	    res
1344	  }

29	/** Mixin that indicates that this is an [[firrtl.annotations.Annotation]] used to generate a [[ChiselOptions]] view.
30	  */
31	sealed trait ChiselOption { this: Annotation => }

33	/** On an exception, this will cause the full stack trace to be printed as opposed to a pruned stack trace.

136	
137	  /** Removes line comments (starting with '#') and trims leading and trailing whitespace
138	    *
139	    * Returns the trimmed String and the number of whitespace characters trimmed from the beginning
140	    */
141	  private def trimAndRemoveComments(s: String): (String, Int) = {
142	    val commentStart = s.indexOf('#')
143	    val noComment = if (commentStart == -1) s else s.splitAt(commentStart)._1 // Only take part before line comment
144	    val trimmed = noComment.trim()
145	    // We still need to calculate how much whitespace was removed for use in error messages
146	    val amountTrimmedFromStart =
147	      trimmed.headOption.map(c => s.indexOf(c)).filter(_ > 0).getOrElse(0)

149	  }

152	  private[chisel3] val filters: Seq[WarningFilter] = {
153	    require(value.exists, s"Warning configuration file '$value' must exist!")
154	    require(value.isFile && value.canRead, s"Warning configuration file '$value' must be a readable file!")
155	    val lines = scala.io.Source.fromFile(value).getLines()
156	    lines.zipWithIndex.flatMap { case (contents, lineNo) =>
157	      val (str, jdx) = trimAndRemoveComments(contents)
158	      Option.when(str.nonEmpty) {
159	        WarningFilter.parse(str) match {
160	          case Right(wf) => wf
161	          case Left((idx, msg)) =>
162	            val carat = (" " * (idx + jdx)) + "^"
163	            val info = s"$value:${lineNo + 1}:$idx" // +1 to lineNo because we start at 0 but files start with 1
164	            // Note tab before value and carat
165	            throw new Exception(
166	              s"Failed to parse configuration at $info: $msg\n  $contents\n  $carat"
167	            )

225	object ChiselGeneratorAnnotation extends HasShellOptions {

227	  /** Try to convert a string to a Scala type. */
228	  private def stringToAny(str: String): Any = {
229	
230	    /* Something that looks like object creation, e.g., "Foo(42)" */
231	    val classPattern = "([a-zA-Z0-9_$.]+)\\((.*)\\)".r
232	
233	    str match {
234	      case boolean if boolean.toBooleanOption.isDefined => boolean.toBoolean
235	      case integer if integer.toIntOption.isDefined     => integer.toInt
236	      case float if float.toDoubleOption.isDefined      => float.toDouble
237	      case classPattern(a, b) =>
238	        val constructor = Class.forName(a).getConstructors()(0)
239	        if (b.isEmpty) {
240	          constructor.newInstance()
241	        } else {
242	          val arguments = b.split(',').map(stringToAny).toSeq
243	          constructor.newInstance(arguments: _*)
244	        }
245	      case string => str
246	    }
247	  }

249	  /** Construct a [[ChiselGeneratorAnnotation]] with a generator function that will try to construct a Chisel Module
250	    * from using that Module's name. The Module must both exist in the class path and not take parameters.
251	    * @param name a module name

253	    * that Module is found

255	  def apply(name: String): ChiselGeneratorAnnotation = {

261	        // Note that this does *not* need to chain with the catches below which are triggered by an
262	        // invalid name or a constructor that takes arguments rather than by the code being run
263	        // itself.
264	        case e: InvocationTargetException =>
265	          throw e.getCause
266	        case e: ClassNotFoundException =>
267	          throw new OptionsException(
268	            s"Unable to run module generator '$name' because it or one of its arguments could not be found. (Did you misspell it or them?)",
269	            e
270	          )
271	        case e: IllegalArgumentException =>
272	          throw new OptionsException(
273	            s"Unable to run module generator '$name' because the arguments are invalid. (Did you pass the correct number and type of arguments?)",

275	          )
276	        case e: ClassCastException =>

280	          )

1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package chisel3
4	
5	import firrtl._
6	import firrtl.options.OptionsView
7	import firrtl.{annoSeqToSeq, seqToAnnoSeq, AnnotationSeq}
8	import chisel3.internal.firrtl.ir.{Circuit => ChiselCircuit}
9	import chisel3.stage.CircuitSerializationAnnotation.FirrtlFileFormat
10	
11	import scala.annotation.nowarn
12	
13	package object stage {
14	
15	  final val pleaseSwitchToCIRCT = deprecatedMFCMessage + " Please switch to circt.stage.ChiselStage."
16	
17	  @nowarn("cat=deprecation&msg=Use elaboratedCircuit instead")
18	  implicit object ChiselOptionsView extends OptionsView[ChiselOptions] {
19	
20	    def view(options: AnnotationSeq): ChiselOptions = options.collect { case a: ChiselOption => a }
21	      .foldLeft(new ChiselOptions()) { (c, x) =>
22	        x match {
23	          case PrintFullStackTraceAnnotation => c.copy(printFullStackTrace = true)
24	          case ThrowOnFirstErrorAnnotation   => c.copy(throwOnFirstError = true)
25	          case WarningsAsErrorsAnnotation =>
26	            c.copy(warningFilters = c.warningFilters :+ WarningsAsErrorsAnnotation.asFilter)
27	          case ChiselOutputFileAnnotation(f) => c.copy(outputFile = Some(f))
28	          case a: ChiselCircuitAnnotation =>
29	            c.copy(elaboratedCircuit = Some(a.elaboratedCircuit))
30	          case SourceRootAnnotation(s) => c.copy(sourceRoots = c.sourceRoots :+ s)
31	          case a: WarningConfigurationAnnotation     => c.copy(warningFilters = c.warningFilters ++ a.filters)
32	          case a: WarningConfigurationFileAnnotation => c.copy(warningFilters = c.warningFilters ++ a.filters)
33	          case UseLegacyWidthBehavior         => c.copy(useLegacyWidth = true)
34	          case RemapLayer(oldLayer, newLayer) => c.copy(layerMap = c.layerMap + ((oldLayer, newLayer)))
35	          case IncludeUtilMetadata            => c.copy(includeUtilMetadata = true)
36	          case UseSRAMBlackbox                => c.copy(useSRAMBlackbox = true)
37	          case IncludeInlineTestsForModuleAnnotation(glob) =>
38	            c.copy(inlineTestIncluder = c.inlineTestIncluder.includeModule(glob))
39	          case IncludeInlineTestsWithNameAnnotation(glob) =>
40	            c.copy(inlineTestIncluder = c.inlineTestIncluder.includeTest(glob))
41	          case SuppressSourceInfoAnnotation => c.copy(suppressSourceInfo = true)
42	        }
43	      }
44	
45	  }
46	}
47

1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package chisel3.internal
4	
5	import java.io.{BufferedWriter, File, FileWriter}
6	import scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer
7	
8	/** Represents a single traced module elaboration event. */
9	private[chisel3] case class TraceEvent(
10	  val parent:     Option[TraceEvent],
11	  val startNanos: Long,
12	  var moduleName: String = "",
13	  var endNanos:   Long = 0
14	)
15	
16	/** Helper to track nested timing information for module elaboration.
17	  *
18	  * This class accumulates nested timing information during Chisel elaboration
19	  * by measuring the time between calls to `pushModule` and `popModule`, which
20	  * should surround elaboration of a single module.
21	  *
22	  * Since this is an internal debugging feature, tracing is not really exposed
23	  * as an option to the user, but instead is enabled when `CHISEL_TRACE_FILE`
24	  * environment variable is set to an output path. The resulting trace file can
25	  * be visualized using [flamegraph] or [inferno]:
26	  *
27	  * ```bash
28	  * # Run Chisel elaboration with environment variable set:
29	  * CHISEL_TRACE_FILE=$PWD/trace.txt <chisel command>
30	  * # Visualize using flamegraph.pl:
31	  * flamegraph.pl trace.txt > trace.svg
32	  * # Visualize using inferno:
33	  * inferno-flamegraph trace.txt > trace.svg
34	  * ```
35	  *
36	  * [flamegraph]: https://github.com/brendangregg/FlameGraph
37	  * [inferno]: https://github.com/jonhoo/inferno
38	  */
39	private[chisel3] class ElaborationTrace {
40	  private val traceFilePath: Option[String] =
41	    sys.props.get("chisel.trace.file").orElse(sys.env.get("CHISEL_TRACE_FILE"))
42	  private val enabled: Boolean = traceFilePath.isDefined
43	
44	  private val events:       ArrayBuffer[TraceEvent] = ArrayBuffer.empty
45	  private var currentEvent: Option[TraceEvent] = None
46	
47	  /** Start a new trace event for a module. This should be called before
48	    * evaluating its constructor. */
49	  def pushModule(): Unit = if (enabled) {
50	    currentEvent = Some(TraceEvent(currentEvent, System.nanoTime()))
51	  }
52	
53	  /** Stop the tracing for a module. This should be called after evaluating its
54	    * constructor. Must have a matching earlier `pushModule` call. */
55	  def popModule(moduleName: String): Unit = if (enabled) {
56	    val event = currentEvent.get
57	    event.moduleName = moduleName
58	    event.endNanos = System.nanoTime()
59	    events += event
60	    currentEvent = event.parent
61	  }
62	
63	  /** Finish tracing and write results to file if tracing is enabled. */
64	  def finish(): Unit = traceFilePath.foreach { path =>
65	    val writer = new BufferedWriter(new FileWriter(new File(path)))
66	    try {
67	      for (event <- events) {
68	        // Build stack by walking parent chain
69	        var stack = List(event.moduleName)
70	        var parent = event.parent
71	        while (parent.isDefined) {
72	          stack = parent.get.moduleName :: stack
73	          parent = parent.get.parent
74	        }
75	        val durationMicros = (event.endNanos - event.startNanos) / 1000
76	        writer.write(s"${stack.mkString(";")} $durationMicros\n")
77	      }
78	    } finally {
79	      writer.close()
80	    }
81	  }
82	}
83

43	        while (i < (sl.line - 1) && lines.hasNext) {
44	          lines.next()

46	        }
47	        val line = lines.next()
48	        val caretLine = (" " * (sl.col - 1)) + "^"
49	        Some(line :: caretLine :: Nil)
50	      } catch {
51	        case scala.util.control.NonFatal(_) => None
52	      }
53	    }
54	    val sourceRootsWithDefault = if (sourceRoots.nonEmpty) sourceRoots else Seq(new File("."))
55	    // View allows us to search the directories one at a time and early out
56	    sourceRootsWithDefault.view.map(tryFileInSourceRoot(_)).collectFirst { case Some(value) => value }.getOrElse(Nil)
57	  }
58	
59	  /** Utility methods that can be added to exceptions.
60	    */
61	  implicit class ThrowableHelpers(throwable: Throwable) {
62	
63	    /** For an exception, mutably trim a stack trace to user code only.
64	      *
65	      * This does the following actions to the stack trace:
66	      *
67	      *   1. From the top, remove elements while the (root) package matches the packageTrimlist
68	      *   2. Optionally, from the bottom, remove elements until the class matches an anchor
69	      *   3. From the anchor (or the bottom), remove elements while the (root) package matches the packageTrimlist
70	      *
71	      * @param packageTrimlist packages that should be removed from the stack trace
72	      * @param anchor an optional class name at which user execution might begin, e.g., a main object
73	      * @return nothing as this mutates the exception directly
74	      */
75	    def trimStackTraceToUserCode(
76	      packageTrimlist: Set[String] = packageTrimlist,
77	      anchor:          Option[String] = Some(builderName)
78	    ): Unit = {
79	      def inTrimlist(ste: StackTraceElement) = {
80	        val packageName = ste.getClassName().takeWhile(_ != '.')
81	        packageTrimlist.contains(packageName)
82	      }
83	
84	      val trimStackTrace =
85	        (a: Array[StackTraceElement]) => {
86	          // Step 1: Remove elements from the top in the package trimlist
87	          //         Only include ellipsis at top if something is dropped from top
88	          val droppedFromTop = inTrimlist(a.head)
89	          val trimmed =
90	            a.dropWhile(inTrimlist)
91	              // Step 2: Optionally remove elements from the bottom until the anchor
92	              .reverse
93	              .dropWhile(ste => anchor.map(b => !ste.getClassName.startsWith(b)).getOrElse(false))
94	              // Step 3: Remove elements from the bottom in the package trimlist
95	              .dropWhile(inTrimlist)
96	              // Step 4: Reverse back to the original order
97	              .reverse
98	              .toArray
99	          // Step 5: Add ellipsis stack trace elements and "--full-stacktrace" info
100	          val withEllipses =
101	            Option.when(droppedFromTop)(ellipsis()) ++:
102	              trimmed :+
103	              ellipsis() :+
104	              ellipsis(
105	                Some("Stack trace trimmed to user code only. Rerun with --full-stacktrace to see the full stack trace")
106	              )
107	          // Step 6: Mutate the stack trace in this exception
108	          throwable.setStackTrace(withEllipses)
109	        }
110	
111	      val stackTrace = throwable.getStackTrace
112	      if (stackTrace.nonEmpty) {
113	        trimStackTrace(stackTrace)
114	      }
115	    }
116	
117	  }
118	}
119	
120	/** Filter for warnings that can suppress, keep as warning, or elevate to error
121	  *
122	  * May filter on source file or message, a None filter means "match everything"
123	  */
124	private[chisel3] case class WarningFilter(
125	  src:    Option[PathMatcher],
126	  id:     Option[WarningID.WarningID],
127	  action: WarningFilter.Action
128	) {
129	
130	  /** Does this filter apply to the warning? */
131	  def applies(warning: Warning): Boolean = {
132	    // Option.forall matches if None which is intentional
133	    val idMatch = this.id.forall(_ == warning.id)
134	    // Using def so that srcMatch won't run unless necessary
135	    def srcMatch = this.src match {
136	      case None => true // No src regex means match all
137	      case Some(srcGlob) =>
138	        val filename = warning.info.filenameOption
139	        filename match {
140	          case Some(filename) => srcGlob.matches(Paths.get(filename))
141	          case None           => false
142	        }
143	    }
144	    idMatch && srcMatch
145	  }
146	}
147	private[chisel3] object WarningFilter {
148	  sealed trait Action
149	  case object Suppress extends Action
150	  case object Warn extends Action
151	  case object Error extends Action
152	
153	  // Some helpers for error reporting
154	  private def actionOneOf = "must be one of ':e, :w, or :s'."
155	  private def categoryOneOf = "must be one of 'any', 'src', or 'id'."
156	
157	  // TODO find a better way to deal with line and column
158	  private def srcGlobDefault(base: String): String = base // s"**/$base"
